name: Laad Bazaar — Bangles and Bridal
description: Laad Bazaar is the most famous traditional market in Hyderabad, a 400-year-old market street adjacent to Charminar specialising in lac bangles, bridal jewellery, and traditional crafts. The market has over 500 shops packed into narrow lanes selling bangles in every colour, material, and design — from plain lac bangles at ten rupees to elaborate gold-plated and stone-set bridal sets worth thousands. Beyond bangles the market sells Hyderabadi attar perfumes, embroidered cloth, pearl jewellery, traditional footwear, and wedding accessories. Bargaining is expected and prices are negotiable. The market is at its most vibrant on evenings and during wedding seasons and Eid.

name: Patthargatti Pearl Market
description: Patthargatti is the wholesale and retail pearl trading street in the heart of Hyderabad's old city, the epicentre of the city's 400-year pearl trade. The street is lined with showrooms and small shops selling natural sea pearls, cultured freshwater pearls, and Basra pearls in every size, shape, and finish. Hyderabadi pearl jewellery — particularly the traditional satlada necklace with seven strands — is among the most prized jewellery in India. Buyers should look for GIA or IGI certified pearls and deal with established shops to ensure authenticity. The market also sells pearl jewellery components for those who wish to create custom pieces.

name: Shilparamam Crafts Emporium
description: Shilparamam in Hitech City is the definitive destination for buying authentic Telangana and Andhra Pradesh handicrafts directly from artisans at government-regulated fair prices. The 65-acre crafts village has over 300 permanent stalls selling Kalamkari hand-painted fabric and garments, Nirmal lacquerware paintings, Kondapalli wooden toys and dolls, Etikoppaka turned wood toys, Bidri metalwork inlaid with silver, Pochampally Ikat textiles, Dharmavaram silk sarees, and Pembarti metal craft. All items are certified authentic and priced fairly without scope for cheating tourists. The complex also has a weekly tribal arts market on Saturdays and Sundays.

name: GVK One Mall — Banjara Hills
description: GVK One is Hyderabad's premier luxury shopping mall located on Road Number 1, Banjara Hills, housing the largest concentration of international luxury brands in the city. The mall features Louis Vuitton, Burberry, Hugo Boss, Michael Kors, Emporio Armani, and over 150 other brands across four floors. The food court and restaurant level includes international dining options. The mall is air-conditioned, secure, and well-maintained with ample parking. It serves as the benchmark for upscale retail in Hyderabad and is located in the heart of the premium Banjara Hills residential and commercial area, convenient to most upscale hotels.
best_time: Afternoon 2pm-6pm on weekdays to avoid weekend crowds

name: Begum Bazaar Wholesale Market
description: Begum Bazaar is the largest wholesale market in Hyderabad and one of the busiest commercial districts in South India, located in the heart of the city near Nampally. The market sells everything from spices, dry fruits, and groceries to textiles, hardware, plastics, and household goods at wholesale prices. The spice section is particularly remarkable — open sacks of dried chillis, turmeric, coriander, and the famous Hyderabadi spice mix for biryani fill the air with an overwhelming fragrance. The adjacent Sultan Bazaar specialises in textiles and readymade garments at very low prices. A sensory overload and an authentic local experience.

name: Pothys and Kalanjali — Traditional Silk and Sarees
description: For traditional Indian textiles, two establishments stand out in Hyderabad. Kalanjali on Road Number 10, Banjara Hills is a multi-floor emporium dedicated to Andhra and Telangana traditional crafts and textiles including Pochampally Ikat sarees, Gadwal silk sarees, Dharmavaram silks, and Kalamkari prints. Pothys in Himayatnagar is a large South Indian textile chain with an extensive collection of Kanjivaram silk sarees, cotton sarees, and dress materials. Both stores have knowledgeable staff who explain the weaving techniques and certify authenticity. Prices range from affordable cottons to expensive heirloom silks.

name: Hyderabad Book Fair and Abids Book Street
description: Hyderabad has a rich literary culture with Abids being the traditional centre of its book trade. The streets around Abids Circle have dozens of bookshops selling new, second-hand, and rare books including out-of-print titles on Hyderabad history, Deccani culture, and Telugu literature. A.A. Hussain and Company is the most famous bookstore, established in 1891 and carrying an enormous range of titles. The annual Hyderabad Book Fair held at the Nizam College Grounds in January draws over a million visitors with hundreds of publishers and offers discounts of 20-50 percent. The area is also excellent for purchasing books on Hyderabad's history.

name: IKEA Hyderabad — Hitech City
description: IKEA's first India store opened in Hyderabad in 2018, located in Hitech City, and remains a significant retail destination. The massive 400,000-square-foot store follows the classic IKEA format with room displays, a Swedish restaurant serving meatballs and lingonberry dishes alongside Indian adaptations, and a marketplace section with thousands of products. For travellers staying in long-term accommodation or those interested in experiencing a cultural retail phenomenon, the Hyderabad IKEA is worth visiting. The store is always busy on weekends and the Swedish restaurant in particular has developed a dedicated local following. Located adjacent to the Hitech City IT district.
best_time: Weekday afternoons to avoid weekend crowds

name: Numaish — All India Industrial Exhibition
description: Numaish is the All India Industrial Exhibition held annually from January 1 to February 15 at the Exhibition Grounds in Nampally, making it one of the longest-running trade fairs in Asia, established in 1938. The exhibition spreads across 45 acres and hosts over 4000 stalls from across India representing every state's crafts, industries, food products, and commercial enterprises. For visitors in Hyderabad between January and mid-February, Numaish is an unmissable event — it is simultaneously a shopping festival, cultural exhibition, food fair, and entertainment event. Entry is cheap and the shopping opportunities range from budget handicrafts to electronics and textiles.
